messages.content: Explain in a short paragraph what the mitochondria does in a cell.

message.content: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell. It generates most of the cell's supply of adenosine triphosphate, used as a source of chemical energy. Beyond ATP production it is involved in signalling, cellular differentiation, and cell death, as well as maintaining control of the cell cycle and cell growth.
